name: Grilled Fish in Banana Leaf
recipeIngredient: 1 large Banana leaf (or more if needed)
recipeInstructions:
1. Make the garlic and pepper paste: In a mini-food processor, add the garlic, peppercorns, coriander root, and ginger. Process into a fine mince. Transfer to a mortar and pound into a smooth paste. Add soy sauce and blend thoroughly.
2. Prepare the fish: Clean the fish and pat dry with paper towels. Rub the garlic mixture over the entire fish and set aside for 30 minutes.
3. Prepare the banana leaf wrapping:
- Choose a banana leaf about 6 inches longer and 3 times wider than the fish.
- Rinse the leaf with cold water to clean it.
- Plunge the leaf into boiling water for a few seconds to soften it, then wipe dry.
4. Assemble the wrapped fish: Place the leaf glossy side down on a work surface. Brush vegetable oil onto the leaf where the fish will lie. Set the marinated fish on the oiled surface and fold over the wide sides, overlapping at the top and securing with toothpicks. Fold the ends to enclose the sides and secure them with toothpicks as well.
5. Grill the wrapped fish: Place the parcel about 3 inches above medium-high heat coals and grill each side for 8-10 minutes. If using trout, marinate the 4 fish together, wrap each individually, and reduce grilling time to about 3 minutes per side.
6. Serve: Open the leaf and serve with the chile lime sauce alongside.
